overview: A two-component web application: a Go backend API handling auth, todos, and categories with an in-memory/relational store, and a React web frontend. The frontend communicates with the backend via REST/JSON over HTTPS using session cookies for authentication.
requirements: Users register with email + strong password (8+ chars, upper/lower/number); passwords hashed with bcrypt.
Login issues 24h session token in HTTP-only secure cookie; 3 failed attempts lock account 15 minutes.
Authenticated users can CRUD todos (title ≤200, description ≤2000, optional ISO8601 due date, optional category, completion status).
Completion timestamp recorded when marking complete; unique ID per todo.
Users can CRUD categories with unique names per user (≤50 chars); deleting a category unassigns it from todos without deleting todos.
Cross-user access to todos/categories is denied and logged.
Filtering by completion status, category, and due date range.
Optimistic locking on concurrent todo updates.
All input sanitized; referential integrity and atomic commits; rollback on failure.
Response <200ms p95 normal; <500ms for users with up to 10,000 todos; 100 concurrent users supported.
All traffic over HTTPS; auth attempts logged with timestamp + IP.

components.name: todo-api
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go backend service using net/http on port 9090. Use an in-memory store (map-based with sync.RWMutex) keyed by userID for users, todos, categories; acceptable for this scope but structure repositories so a relational DB (Postgres) could be swapped in. Endpoints under /api/v1 plus /health returning 200. Auth: POST /auth/register validates email regex and password (≥8 chars, at least one upper, one lower, one digit), hashes with golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt. POST /auth/login verifies hash; track failed attempts per account — after 3 consecutive failures lock for 15 min (return 423). On success set HTTP-only Secure SameSite=Lax cookie 'session' containing a random 32-byte token, 24h TTL, store token→userID server-side. POST /auth/logout clears cookie and invalidates token. Password reset: /auth/password-reset/request generates token (log it; email sending stubbed) and /confirm updates password after validating token. Middleware requires valid session cookie for all /todos and /categories routes; returns 401 otherwise. Todos: enforce title required & ≤200, description ≤2000, dueDate ISO-8601 parsed via time.RFC3339, optional categoryId (must belong to same user, else 400). When completed flips false→true set completedAt=now. Use a 'version' integer field; PUT requires matching version else 409. DELETE hard-removes. GET supports filters completed, categoryId, dueFrom, dueTo. Categories: name unique per user (case-insensitive), max 50. DELETE sets categoryId=null on all referencing todos of that user. Rename via PUT. Log all auth attempts with timestamp, email, IP (X-Forwarded-For or RemoteAddr). Sanitize strings (reject NUL bytes, trim). Enable permissive CORS for the frontend origin with credentials. Return JSON errors {error: string}. Ensure /health is public. Include Dockerfile with multi-stage golang build → distroless/scratch, EXPOSE 9090.

components.name: todo-web
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Todo Web (static)
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      responses:
        '200': {description: OK}
components.componentAgentInstructions: Build a React + TypeScript SPA using Vite. Pages: Register, Login, Forgot Password, Reset Password, Todos dashboard (list with filters by completion/category/due date range, create/edit/delete forms), Categories management. Use fetch with credentials:'include' to call the todo-api base URL from env var VITE_API_BASE (default /api/v1). Store no auth tokens in JS (rely on HTTP-only cookie); infer login state from a /me endpoint or local flag after login success. Show validation errors from API. Support marking todo complete (sends completed=true + version). Use react-router for routing. Style minimally with CSS or Tailwind. Serve the built static files via nginx on port 8080. Include a /health location in nginx config returning 200 'ok'. Provide Dockerfile: node build stage then nginx:alpine copying dist/ to /usr/share/nginx/html, with nginx.conf enabling SPA fallback (try_files $uri /index.html) and /health.
